{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142/events","html_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142","id":2640226523,"node_id":"I_kwDOADqB3M6dXqjb","number":14142,"title":"[FEATURE] Make CodeRunner and python code loading configurable","user":{"login":"levsa","id":310653,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMxMDY1Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/310653?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa","html_url":"https://github.com/levsa","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":1310697832,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzEwNjk3ODMy","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/labels/TRIAGE","name":"TRIAGE","color":"e2e2e2","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-07T08:14:37Z","updated_at":"2024-11-07T08:14:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Problem description

When running tests, I want to use stubs or mocks when running the production code, and verify stuff afterwards. `DirectoryHandler` instantiating a `CodeRunner` which loads the production code makes this very hard, a workaround is to use `monkeypatch` and override with my own `CodeRunner` which monkeypatches stuff after loading.

### Feature description

A first step would be if one could choose the type of `CodeRunner` to be instansiated, or if it was created with a method in `DirectoryHandler` which could be overridden. 

I'm not sure what the next step would be, to make production code loaded by `CodeRunner` more testable. My current solution is to `monkeypatch` factory methods that create stubs instead of real services, in my own `CodeRunner.run` method.

### Potential alternatives

Another option might be to use an injector framework (like e.g. [injector](https://pypi.org/project/injector/) ) , where one can bind ones own kind of subclasses.

### Additional information

My code runner that can inject stubs in the loaded code:

```
    def database_service_stub():
        with Stub(DatabaseService) as service_stub:
            service_stub.get_users().returns[{'name': 'test_user'})
        return service_stub

    def create_injector():
        injector = Injector()
        injector.binder.bind(DatabaseService, to=database_service_stub())
        return injector

    class MyCodeRunner(CodeRunner):
        def __init__(
            self,
            source: str,
            path: PathLike,
            argv: list[str],
            package: ModuleType | None = None,
        ) -> None:
            super().__init__(source, path, argv, package=package)

        def run(self, module: ModuleType, post_check: Callable[[], None] | None = None) -> None:
            super().run(module, functools.partial(self.test_setup, module, post_check))

        def test_setup(
            self, module: ModuleType, post_check: Callable[[], None] | None = None
        ) -> None:
            monkeypatch.setattr(module, 'create_injector', create_injector_stub)
            post_check()
```

The code runner is monkeypatched like so (a bit ugly and easily broken):

```
        monkeypatch_session.setattr(
            bokeh.application.handlers.server_lifecycle, "CodeRunner", MyCodeRunner
        )

```


My loaded bokeh code would then have (`main.py`):

```
class BokehApp:
    @inject
    def __init__(self, document: Document, database_service: DatabaseService) -> None:
        self.users = database_service.get_users()
     ...   

injector = create_injector()
injector.binder.bind(Document, to=curdoc())
injector.get(BokehApp)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

When running tests, I want to use stubs or mocks when running the production code, and verify stuff afterwards. `DirectoryHandler` instantiating a `CodeRunner` which loads the production code makes this very hard, a workaround is to use `monkeypatch` and override with my own `CodeRunner` which monkeypatches stuff after loading.

### Feature description

A first step would be if one could choose the type of `CodeRunner` to be instansiated, or if it was created with a method in `DirectoryHandler` which could be overridden. Also, it would make test setup easier if `CodeRunner` called a custom `prepare` or `post_load` method in the `CodeRunner` class (empty in base class), that would make it easy to override and inject behavior.

I'm not sure what the next step would be, to make production code loaded by `CodeRunner` more testable. My current solution is to `monkeypatch` factory methods that create stubs instead of real services, in my own `CodeRunner.run` method.

### Potential alternatives

Another option might be to use an injector framework (like e.g. [injector](https://pypi.org/project/injector/) ) , where one can bind ones own kind of subclasses.

### Additional information

My code runner that can inject stubs in the loaded code:

```
    def database_service_stub():
        with Stub(DatabaseService) as service_stub:
            service_stub.get_users().returns([{'name': 'test_user'}])
        return service_stub

    def create_test_injector():
        injector = Injector()
        injector.binder.bind(DatabaseService, to=database_service_stub())
        return injector

    class MyCodeRunner(CodeRunner):
        def __init__(
            self,
            source: str,
            path: PathLike,
            argv: list[str],
            package: ModuleType | None = None,
        ) -> None:
            super().__init__(source, path, argv, package=package)

        def run(self, module: ModuleType, post_check: Callable[[], None] | None = None) -> None:
            super().run(module, functools.partial(self.test_setup, module, post_check))

        def test_setup(
            self, module: ModuleType, post_check: Callable[[], None] | None = None
        ) -> None:
            monkeypatch.setattr(module, 'create_injector', create_test_injector)
            post_check()
```

The code runner is monkeypatched like so (a bit ugly and easily broken):

```
        monkeypatch_session.setattr(
            bokeh.application.handlers.server_lifecycle, "CodeRunner", MyCodeRunner
        )

```


My loaded bokeh code would then have (`main.py`):

```
class BokehApp:
    @inject
    def __init__(self, document: Document, database_service: DatabaseService) -> None:
        self.document = document
        self.users = database_service.get_users()
     ...   

injector = create_injector()
injector.binder.bind(Document, to=curdoc())
injector.get(BokehApp)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/comments/2466896388","html_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142#issuecomment-2466896388","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14142","id":2466896388,"node_id":"IC_kwDOADqB3M6TCdoE","user":{"login":"levsa","id":310653,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMxMDY1Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/310653?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa","html_url":"https://github.com/levsa","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/levsa/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-10T20:12:06Z","updated_at":"2024-11-10T20:12:06Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> @levsa I don't have a strong sense of your use-case and requirements which is a disincentive for me to work on anything (risk of spending effort only to make the wrong thing). However, you seem you have a grasp of exactly what would be useful to you, so it would probably make much more sense for you to submit a PR for this, and then we can hammer out any details in the PR (it's much more efficient to evaluate and iterate on a concrete first proposal)

Sure, sounds like a good idea!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/comments/2466896388/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Software versions

Python version: 3.11.4
Bokeh version: 3.2.1
mypy version: 1.4.1

### Browser name and version

_No response_

### Jupyter notebook / Jupyter Lab version

_No response_

### Expected behavior

I am receiving a stubborn mypy error when setting the font of my plot titles.

### Observed behavior

error: "Property[Any]" has no attribute "text_font"

### Example code

```Python
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show
from typing import Any

plot_settings: dict[str, Any] = {
	'height': 300,
	'toolbar_location': None,
	'width': 300,
	'x_range': (-1., 1.),
	'y_range': (-1., 1.),
}
truth_fig = figure(title='Ground Truth', **plot_settings)
truth_fig.title.text_font = truth_fig.axis.major_label_text_font = 'CMU serif'
plot_settings = {'fill_color': '#ffffff', 'line_color': '#101010', 'x': 0., 'y': 0.}
truth_fig.circle(radius=0.5, **plot_settings)
show(truth_fig)
```

### Expected behavior

mypy check to pass

### Observed behavior

mypy finds an error:

```
error: Argument 1 to "gridplot" has incompatible type "list[figure]"; expected "list[list[Any | None]]"  [arg-type]
```

### Example code

```Python
from bokeh import layouts
from bokeh.plotting import figure

figures = [figure(title="figure_1"), figure(title="figure_2")]
grid = layouts.column(layouts.gridplot(figures, ncols=3), data_table)
```


### Stack traceback or browser console output

Proposed fix:

```python
@overload
def gridplot(
    children: list[UIElement | None],
    *,
    sizing_mode: SizingModeType | None = None,
    toolbar_location: LocationType | None = "above",
    ncols: int,
    width: int | None = None,
    height: int | None = None,
    toolbar_options: dict[ToolbarOptions, Any] | None = None,
    merge_tools: bool = True,
) -> GridPlot:
    ...


@overload
def gridplot(
    children: list[list[UIElement | None]],
    *,
    sizing_mode: SizingModeType | None = None,
    toolbar_location: LocationType | None = "above",
    ncols: None,
    width: int | None = None,
    height: int | None = None,
    toolbar_options: dict[ToolbarOptions, Any] | None = None,
    merge_tools: bool = True,
) -> GridPlot:
    ...


def gridplot(
    children: list[UIElement | None] | list[list[UIElement | None]],
    *,
    sizing_mode: SizingModeType | None = None,
    toolbar_location: LocationType | None = "above",
    ncols: int | None = None,
    width: int | None = None,
    height: int | None = None,
    toolbar_options: dict[ToolbarOptions, Any] | None = None,
    merge_tools: bool = True,
) -> GridPlot:
    if ncols:
    
    # implementation here
